Dear Mr. Cameron: 
Deceillber 16, 1975 
/ 
Re: 
Request for Opinion 
No. 75-213 
Thank you for your letter of December 4, 1975, re-
questing an opinion relative to whether you have a conflict 
of interest which would preclude you from voting on the 
issue of establishing a special task force. 
Since your 
opinion request does not raise a novel question 8f inter-
pretation under the Political Reform Act, no formal 
opinion will be issued. 
According to the facts you outline in your letter, 
the potential conflict of interest about which you are 
concerned involves a business transaction you had with 
Mr. Rupp. 
You state that the transaction was concluded 
on April 23, 1973, and that you have not done any work for 
Mr. Rupp since that time. 
You also state that you do not 
kno"'l whether you will do more work for Hr. Rupp in the 
future. 
These facts do not constitute a conflict of 
interest under the Political Reform Act since your income 
from Mr. Rupp terminated more than bvelve months ago. 
Government Code Section 87103(c). 
I caution you, however, that this informal advice 
does not represent a formal opinion of the COITUuission. 
Moreover, the advice is based on the limited facts pre-
sented by you and is offered solely in response to those 
facts. 
If you wish to request a review by the full Commis-
sion of the denial of your opinion request, you may do so 
pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18321. 
If you have 
any questions, please contact Kenneth Finney, Director of 
the COITUr:1ission's Legal Division.

RE: 
REQUEST FOR OPINION -
CONFLICT OF INTEREST 
Gentlemen: 
This request for your formal opinion is made pursuant 
to the provisions of Government Code §83l1. 
I am a member of 
the Board of Supervisors of the County of Butte. 
He presently 
have before the Board the issue of whether or not to establish 
a special task force in the district attorney's office to investigate 
illegal lot splits. 
Tnis unit, if established, would investigate 
certain land transactions that have occurred throughout the count:y. 
which investigations would lead to pro ecutions, levying of civil 
penal ties and fines, etc. on people vlho \Vere engaged in violations 
of the State Map Act. 
During the discussion of this matter before 
the Board of Supervisors, one of my fellmv board members expressed 
concern as to vlhether or not I \vould have a conflict of interest in 
voting on this issue. 
This issue has become a very heated and 
controversial matter before the Board of Supervisors, and as a 
result of this other board member's contentions, I have abstained 
so far in discussion of or voting on the issue. 
I might add 
parenthetically like many issues that have come before the Board, 
this one seems to come up 1tleekly \vithout being finally resolved, 
and I would further anticipate that 
situation must be resolved 
in that another member, the chairman, has also had the same 
allegation made against him by this other board member. 
In order 
for this issue to be resolved finally, assuming that the task force 
unit is established, my vote or the other board member who has been 
alleged to have the conflict or interest is going to be necessary 
in order to effect a trans 
from tne reserve for contingency. 
I 
am sure you are avlare of the fact th2.t in order to remove funds from 
the reserve for contingency that four affirmative votes are required.

sections.question: Whether Ward Cameron, a Butte County Supervisor, has a conflict of interest which would preclude him from voting on the issue of establishing a special task force in the district attorney's office to investigate illegal lot splits, given that he had performed advertising and public relations work for Mr. Scott Rupp from February 16, 1972 through April 23, 1973.
sections.conclusion: These facts do not constitute a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act since your income from Mr. Rupp terminated more than twelve months ago. Government Code Section 87103(c). However, this informal advice does not represent a formal opinion of the Commission and is based on the limited facts presented.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a county supervisor have a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act when voting on a task force to investigate illegal land divisions, given that the supervisor previously performed independent contractor work for a business owner who may be subject to investigation, with that work relationship ending more than 12 months prior?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: No conflict of interest exists under the Political Reform Act because the supervisor's income from Mr. Rupp ended more than 12 months before the vote, satisfying the requirements of Government Code Section 87103(c), though this constitutes informal advice only.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination of a formal opinion request, but with informal advice provided. The document includes both the incoming request letter from Cameron (dated December 4, 1975) and the FPPC response letter (dated December 16, 1975). The response cites Government Code Section 87103(c) regarding the 12-month rule for past income relationships.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Ward Cameron, a Butte County Supervisor, has a conflict of interest which would preclude him from voting on the issue of establishing a special task force in the district attorney's office to investigate illegal lot splits, given that he had performed advertising and public relations work for Mr. Scott Rupp from February 16, 1972 through April 23, 1973.

CONCLUSION: These facts do not constitute a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act since your income from Mr. Rupp terminated more than twelve months ago. Government Code Section 87103(c). However, this informal advice does not represent a formal opinion of the Commission and is based on the limited facts presented.

embedding.summary: FPPC responded to Supervisor Ward Cameron's request for an opinion regarding a potential conflict of interest in voting on establishing a special task force, finding no conflict under the Political Reform Act since his prior business relationship with a potentially affected party had ended over 12 months earlier.
